newsSince: MATERIAL: Apollo Global Management confirmed on/before the June 19 PUSU deadline that it will NOT make a firm offer (conditional 885p cash + 16.1p dividend proposal revealed May 22). Shares fell ~9-10% to ~751p; Apollo is barred from re-approaching for six months under the Takeover Code. The takeover floor that anchored the prior 'up' asymmetry case is now removed.

sources.label: Bodycote shares fall ~9% as Apollo walks away (Proactive Investors)
Bodycote reaffirms standalone strategy as Apollo walks away (TipRanks)
Apollo backs out of $2bn Bodycote pursuit (Reuters via Investing.com)

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent-node screen: prior 'up' was built on a live 885p bid marking intrinsic value ~25% above market. Apollo formally walked away by Jun 19 and is locked out 6 months, removing that floor. Thermal-processing AI thesis intact but the deal-driven asymmetry that earned the upgrade is gone. Down-rank.
verdicts.bull: Apollo's 885p approach validated deep value; thermal-processing thesis (liquid loops, robotics, automation) untouched.
verdicts.neutral: Standalone story unchanged; trades near pre-bid level with no operational deterioration.
verdicts.bear: Bid floor removed, no re-approach for 6 months; M&A optionality that drove the upgrade has evaporated.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Renewed bid interest after the 6-month lockout or self-help margin recovery.
verdicts.primaryRisk: De-rating now the takeover premium is gone; cyclical end-market softness.
